Artist Statement 1: I went for long run when I was feeling desperate. Sometimes (before I get my head on straight) I feel like my wife and I are just too different. And it feels very clandestined. But I realized that only when things are easy do I slip into easy habits, and slip out of the ones I need to be doing to stay healthy. If there is not enough desperation to turn this big ship, it will stay going the wrong direction. So I realized how important desperation is. How important it is to have pain and discomfort, because that is what teaches me to live better, when nothing else seems to be strong enough to get me out of my ruts.
Story Title again: Poem: These Tombstones Will Cry Out
excerpt:
Hobbling toward your cross, among shouts,
You said 'If they do not praise me, these stones will cry out."

I question, "But honestly, really, would they?"
That sounds like another exaggeration you would say,
like "you can move mountains," or "do greater things than these."
Ones that make me wonder if it gives pause to anyone but me.

As I hobble
to believe 
in you, 
It feels like you
turn your back 
on me 
as you say, 
"Let's just see 
if you can still 
believe in me 
after I 
say this."

But...

I found out today tombstones could be seen from your strides.
And tombstones broke open when you died.
Perhaps when praising mouths fell shut as you fell limp,
these stones cried out, choirs of cracking flint.
And not only stones, but rattling bones took in gasps,
And cried out hosanna, the king comes at last.
...

Sometimes I wonder if the pause, is a briefly cracked door.
And your back turning is inviting me to more
"Let's just see what you can be
when you follow me..."
To where not only the greatest exaggerations are true,
But true in ways that are phenomenally new.

Raw Spoon, April 13, 2025 (Palm Sunday)
Blog excerpt:
At the risk of sounding heretical I will dare to say this.

I have found that one of the most important spiritual disciplines is rest.

Like all disciplines in life, it is incomplete without the others, but if you find that when you try to pray or study you inevitably fall asleep, then your body is probably less fit to exhibit the finer effects that the other disciplines work to bring out in us.

When your body's more basic needs are met, it will be far easier to be patient, loving, generous and kind, for when your own health is provided for then it is more natural for it to flow over to others.

Raw Spoon
Research 1- link:
https://enduringword.com/bible-commentary/Psalm 23

A psalm of David.

1 The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.
2     He makes me lie down in green pastures,
he leads me beside quiet waters,
3     he refreshes my soul.
He guides me along the right paths
    for his name's sake.
4 Even though I walk
    through the darkest valley,[a]
I will fear no evil,
    for you are with me;
your rod and your staff,
    they comfort me.

5 You prepare a table before me
    in the presence of my enemies.
You anoint my head with oil;
    my cup overflows.
6 Surely your goodness and love will follow me
    all the days of my life,
and I will dwell in the house of the Lord
    forever.

Footnotes
a. Psalm 23
3dNotes2: Gilgal: Where Saul went to sacrifice to the Lord and later defeated the Ammonites.
3dNotes3: Mt. Carmel: view from Mt. Carmel (referenced in the Samuel reading today)
